---
title: "RMSD_clustering_acyl_lengths"
format: html
---

```{r}
#install.packages("bio3d")  # if not already installed
library(bio3d)

# Load necessary packages
library(here)
library(stringr)
library(fs)

# Define input and output directories based on your folder structure
input_dir <- here(
  "Docking",
  "AutoDockVina",
  "acyl_tail_lengths",
  "autodock_pdbqt_outputs"
)           # Subfolder with your pdbqt files

output_dir <- here(
  "Docking",
  "AutoDockVina",
  "acyl_tail_lengths",
  "output_pdbqts_converted_to_pdbs"
)

# Create the output folder if it doesn't exist
dir_create(output_dir)

# List all pdbqt files in the input folder
pdbqt_files <- dir_ls(input_dir, glob = "*.pdbqt")

# Function to split a single pdbqt file into separate pdb files
split_pdbqt_to_pdb <- function(file_path, output_dir) {
  # Read the file
  lines <- readLines(file_path)
  
  # Find lines where models start
  model_start <- which(str_detect(lines, "^MODEL"))
  model_end <- which(str_detect(lines, "^ENDMDL"))
  
  # Loop through models
  for (i in seq_along(model_start)) {
    model_lines <- lines[model_start[i]:model_end[i]]
    
    # Create an output file name
    base_name <- path_file(file_path) %>% str_remove("\\.pdbqt$")
    pdb_file <- path(output_dir, paste0(base_name, "_model", i, ".pdb"))
    
    # Remove "MODEL" and "ENDMDL" lines for cleaner pdb
    model_lines <- model_lines[!str_detect(model_lines, "^(MODEL|ENDMDL)")]
    
    # Write PDB
    writeLines(model_lines, pdb_file)
  }
}

# Loop through all pdbqt files and convert
for (file in pdbqt_files) {
  split_pdbqt_to_pdb(file, output_dir)
}

message("All pdbqt files have been converted to PDBs in 'output_pdbqts_converted_to_pdbs' folder.")

# List all PDB files
pdb_files <- dir_ls(output_dir, glob = "*.pdb")

# Count them to check - should have 630 because 10 runs x 9 models in each run x 7 inhibitors = 630
num_pdbs <- length(pdb_files)
num_pdbs
```

```{r}

library(here)
library(dplyr)
library(stringr)
library(fs)

# Path to output PDBs
output_dir <- here(
  "Docking",
  "AutoDockVina",
  "acyl_tail_lengths",
  "output_pdbqts_converted_to_pdbs"
)

# Read all PDB files
pdb_files <- dir_ls(output_dir, glob = "*.pdb")

# Make dataframe and extract Ctail, run, and model
file_df_acyl_lengths <- data.frame(
  file = pdb_files,
  stringsAsFactors = FALSE
) %>%
  mutate(
    # Inhibitor number (Ctail 0–6)
    inhibitor = as.integer(str_extract(basename(file), "(?<=paLpxH_)\\d+(?=Ctail)")),
    
    # Run repeat (1–10)
    run_repeat = as.integer(str_extract(basename(file), "(?<=Ctail_)\\d+")),
    
    # Model number (pose)
    model = as.integer(str_extract(basename(file), "(?<=_model)\\d+")),
    
    # Optional: unique label combining inhibitor + run for plotting
    inhibitor_run = paste0("Ctail", inhibitor, "_run", run_repeat)
  )



```

```{r}
###cluster

library(bio3d)
library(cluster)

inhibitors <- unique(file_df_acyl_lengths$inhibitor)

cluster_inhibitor <- function(inhibitor_id, hcutoff = 2.0) {
  
#get all the file names that belong to one specific inhibitor type (eg. no tail, 1 carbon tail, 2 carbon tails etc)
  files <- file_df_acyl_lengths$file[file_df_acyl_lengths$inhibitor == inhibitor_id]
  
#read the files extracted above as pdbs 
  pdbs <- lapply(files, read.pdb)
  
  #From each stored pdb, extract the 3D atomic coordinates and store in lig_coords
  lig_coords <- lapply(pdbs, function(pdb) pdb$xyz)
  
#combine the coordinates of each pose into a matrix, with each structure (pose) as a row
  xyz <- do.call(rbind, lig_coords)
#compute pairwise RMSD between all poses; we are asking how different is each pose from every other pose in 3D space? 
  rd <- rmsd(xyz)

#converts matrix to a distance objecta nd builds a dendogram (hierarchical cluster) which groups the inhibitor poses across all 10 runs based on how similar they are
  hc <- hclust(as.dist(rd), method = "average")
  
#sets how similar poses must be to count as the same cluster. Poses that differ by ≤ 2 Å RMSD are considered the same cluster.
  clusters <- cutree(hc, h = hcutoff)
  
#From each dominant cluster, represent one dominant pose
  pam_fit <- pam(as.dist(rd), k = length(unique(clusters)))
  medoid_files <- files[pam_fit$medoids]
  
#create list of important clustering results for one inhibitor in one place
  list(
    hc = hc, #store full hierarchical clustering tree in thsi list for later
    clusters = clusters, #store whcih cluster each pose belongs to
    cluster_sizes = table(clusters), #count no. of poses in each cluster and store
    medoid_files = medoid_files #store file names of most representative pose (the medoid) of each cluster
  )
  
}

results_list <- list()
for (inh in unique(file_df_acyl_tails$inhibitor)) {
  results_list[[as.character(inh)]] <- cluster_inhibitor(inh, hcutoff = 2.0)
}

# Initialize an empty list to hold rows
dominant_medoid_rows <- list()

# Loop over each inhibitor
for (inh in names(results_list)) {
  
  # Get cluster sizes for this inhibitor
  cluster_sizes <- results_list[[inh]]$cluster_sizes
  
  # Find the cluster with the maximum size
  dominant_cluster <- as.numeric(names(cluster_sizes)[which.max(cluster_sizes)])
  
  # Get the medoid file corresponding to that cluster
  dominant_medoid <- results_list[[inh]]$medoid_files[dominant_cluster]
  
  # Create a row
  df <- data.frame(
    inhibitor = as.numeric(inh),
    dominant_cluster = dominant_cluster,
    medoid_file = dominant_medoid,
    stringsAsFactors = FALSE
  )
  
  # Add to the list
  dominant_medoid_rows[[inh]] <- df
}

# Combine all rows into one data frame
dominant_medoid_df <- do.call(rbind, dominant_medoid_rows)

# Preview#The numbers in the dominant_cluster column represent which cluster each inhibitor’s poses belong to in the hierarchical clustering analysis.
dominant_medoid_df

# Extract coordinates of dominant medoids
medoid_coords <- lapply(dominant_medoid_df$medoid_file, function(f) {
  pdb <- read.pdb(f)
  xyz <- pdb$xyz
  # Optional: add a column indicating the file/inhibitor
  data.frame(file = basename(f), xyz = I(list(xyz)))
})

# Combine all medoid coordinates into a single data frame
medoid_coords_df <- do.call(rbind, medoid_coords)

# Define correct folder (updated path + correct folder name)
medoid_folder <- here(
  "Docking",
  "AutoDockVina",
  "acyl_tail_lengths",
  "medoid_files_acyls"
)

# Create the folder if it doesn't exist
dir_create(medoid_folder)

# Copy all medoid files into this folder
file.copy(
  dominant_medoid_df$medoid_file,
  file.path(medoid_folder, basename(dominant_medoid_df$medoid_file))
)
```

```{r}
#deal w this bit later!!! idek if i need graphical stuff not sure
plot(results_all$Inhibitor_2$hc,
     main = "Inhibitor 2 RMSD Clustering",
     xlab = "Docked poses",
     sub = "",
     cex = 0.6)

library(dendextend)

abline(h = 2.0, col = "red", lty = 2)

library(dendextend)

dend <- as.dendrogram(results_all$Inhibitor_2$hc)

dend <- color_branches(
  dend,
  k = length(unique(results_all$Inhibitor_2$clusters))
)

plot(dend,
     main = "Inhibitor 2 Clustered Poses",
     cex = 0.6)

barplot(results_all$Inhibitor_2$cluster_sizes,
        main = "Cluster Sizes — Inhibitor 2",
        xlab = "Cluster",
        ylab = "Number of poses")


num_clusters <- sapply(results_all, function(res)
  length(unique(res$clusters))
)

barplot(num_clusters,
        main = "Number of Pose Clusters per Inhibitor",
        xlab = "Inhibitor",
        ylab = "Number of clusters")
```
